nodes.context: New York City street gang with mostly Italian American membership, active from the 1950s to the 2000s.

edges.evidence_quote: Some of the gangs that made up the original South Brooklyn Boys were the South Brooklyn Devils, the Garfield Place Boys, the SB Angels, SB Diapers, the Wanderers, the Degraw St boys, the Sackett St Boys, the Butler Gents, the Gowanus Boys, the Kane St. Midgets, The Little Gents, and the Young Savages.
edges.context: South Brooklyn Devils was a constituent gang that made up the original South Brooklyn Boys.

edges.evidence_quote: A small but fierce gang known as the "Jokers" was a bitter rival, led by a man known as "Cannonball." The two groups fought many times.
edges.context: The Jokers were bitter rivals of South Brooklyn Boys and fought many times.

edges.evidence_quote: Also bitter enemies of SBB were the Puerto Rican Gangs, The Untouchable Bishops, and the Apaches, and the African American gangs The Mau Maus, and the Chaplains.
edges.context: Untouchable Bishops were bitter enemies of South Brooklyn Boys.

edges.evidence_quote: Also bitter enemies of SBB were the Puerto Rican Gangs, The Untouchable Bishops, and the Apaches, and the African American gangs The Mau Maus, and the Chaplains.

edges.evidence_quote: The gang has been loosely affiliated with and has worked for the Italian-American Mafia throughout its history to the present, but it is not an official Mafia crew.
edges.context: South Brooklyn Boys has been loosely affiliated with and worked for the Italian-American Mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Italian-American Mafia , Tanglewood Boys , Ozone Park Boys , Gallo Crew (early)
edges.context: Tanglewood Boys are listed as allies of South Brooklyn Boys in the infobox.
